findings.evidence: `sqrtTower` is defined at lines 46-47 and `rg` finds only `sqrtTower_zero`, `sqrtTower_succ`, `sqrtTower_root_mem`, `sqrtTower_sq_mem`, and `sqrtTower_sup_adjoin_eq_of_mem`, with no `sqrtTower_le_iff`/range bridge; roadmap `Targets.lean` lines 42-45 states the degree target for `IntermediateField.adjoin K (Set.range r)` over arbitrary finite `ι`.
findings.fix: Add characteristic lemmas such as `sqrtTower_le_iff : sqrtTower root n ≤ F ↔ ∀ j, j < n → root j ∈ F`, plus the corresponding equality/bridge lemma for `Fin n` or finite-index `Set.range` needed by the roadmap target.
findings.issue: `sqrtTower` is exported without its universal property, so users cannot prove maps/equalities out of the tower API-style; they must unfold the `adjoin K (root '' Set.Iio n)` body.

findings.evidence: Line 50 has `@[simp]` on `sqrtTower_zero`, while line 55 declares `sqrtTower_succ` without an attribute; `rg -n "\[simp\]|sqrtTower_succ"` shows no simp annotation for the successor lemma.
findings.fix: Mark `sqrtTower_succ` as `@[simp]` if it is intended as the canonical recursive form, or provide a separate simp-normal-form lemma with that rewrite direction.
findings.issue: The successor equation is the normal form for the recursive tower but is not marked for automation, so `simp` will normalize `sqrtTower root 0` but not `sqrtTower root (n+1)`.

summary: The core descent statement is now generic, but the public tower API is still too thin for the stated Layer-0 degree target without unfolding `sqrtTower` or reproving its universal property. Add the missing characteristic lemmas and automation annotations before downstream code depends on this interface.
